summary: iter-96 verified end-to-end. Backend 14/14 NEW pytest cases PASS + iter-95 regression 17/17 PASS. Playwright UI flow: all 7 FR assertions + 5 admin-regression assertions PASS. Branch scope on /api/admin/restaurant/takeaway-pendency and /api/admin/restaurant/orders works for FR + admin ?as_mess_id. FR cross-branch collect → 403 'Pendency not in your branch'. FR /admin/messes POST/PUT both return 403 ('Admin only', NOT 422). UI: FR /admin/messes & /admin/franchise-onboarding redirect to /admin/kitchen-radius, no toast, no spurious 'Add a mess' button, no Messes-in-sidebar, branch pill is disabled (cursor-default, no chevron, no popover on click).
critical_code_review_comments: app_cms.collect_takeaway: FR-vs-rec.user_id mess membership check uses messes.owner_user_id → user.mess_id — correctly aligned with the rest of the codebase's effective_mess_id resolver, so consistent. Good explicit 403 message 'Pendency not in your branch' (testable).

restaurant_orders.admin_orders uses _users_in_mess(mid) — returns None for global, list for branch; the {'user_id':{'$in':[...]}} pattern correctly returns 0 rows when no users live in that branch (e.g., Yavatmal). Verified with a freshly seeded MESS_B that has no orders.

AdminMesses.jsx load() early-returns for franchise_owner BEFORE the GET fires — confirmed no Failed-to-load toast and no 401/403 in network. Navigate redirect is placed AFTER hooks (rule-of-hooks safe).
AdminLayout pill: disabled+cursor-default+no-chevron all gated by role !== 'franchise_owner'. Click is a true no-op (verified — popover count remained 0 after force-click).

Note (pre-existing, not iter-96): the assign-owner endpoint is PATCH not POST. Spec mentioned POST; tests accept 403/404/405 for POST and assert PATCH→403 strictly. Main agent may want to add a POST alias if the doc has been published externally.
test_credentials: Seeded TEST_IT96_* (backend pytest) and TEST_IT96UI_* (Playwright UI) — all cleaned up. Production user user_bb39434e1a5c remains owner of efoodcare-amravati. efoodcare-yavatmal mess kept in DB for future multi-branch tests (no real data attached).
seed_data_creation: Backend fixture creates admin + FR + 2 subscribers (in-mess Amravati, out-of-mess Yavatmal) + 2 restaurant_orders + 3 takeaway_pendency rows (2 in Amravati, 1 in Yavatmal for cross-branch test). UI seed creates separate TEST_IT96UI_* admin + FR sessions, deleted on completion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter96_branch_scope.py is self-contained (own fixture, own teardown, doesn't depend on iter-95 fixture). It overlaps MESS_A=efoodcare-amravati ownership during the test, so DO NOT run iter-95 + iter-96 in the same pytest invocation without --forked or separate processes (both rebind owner_user_id). Tested in isolation both passed cleanly. UI seed data uses session cookies (HttpOnly) — to impersonate a user, set cookie domain=dining-pass-scan.preview.emergentagent.com, path=/, sameSite=None, secure=True. Branch pill desktop selector: [data-testid='branch-pill-desktop']; popover: [data-testid='branch-switcher-popover'].
rca of the issue: User-reported triple bug was caused by: (1) AdminMesses/Onboarding rendered HQ-only controls for franchise_owner without redirect or guard — fixed with Navigate redirect + load() early-return; (2) /admin/restaurant/takeaway-pendency had NO branch filter — now uses _users_in_mess() filter; (3) /admin/restaurant/orders also had NO branch filter — same fix. All three are addressed and verified. The branch pill UX (clickable but redundant for FR) was hardened to read-only badge.
